title: Crochet: Alex Cora, Red Sox coaches ‘paid the cost of our own crime’ with firing
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Red Sox "Ultimately, I blame myself a lot for where we’re at this year.

Scott Taetsch/Getty Images By Conor Ryan April 27, 2026 | 7:08 PM 2 minutes to read The mood in the Red Sox’ visiting clubhouse in Baltimore on Sunday was one of confusion, anger, and sadness — an expected sentiment after Boston fired Alex Cora and several coaches the previous evening.

title: Lynn man arrested for human trafficking
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Local News Miguel Cabrera faces felony charges of trafficking a person for sexual servitude and deriving support from prostitution.
By Carson Lyle April 27, 2026 | 6:52 PM 1 minute to read A Lynn man was arrested last week on a human trafficking and prostitution warrent, police said.

Miguel Cabrera, 34, was arrested Wednesday on a warrant issued from South Boston District Court for trafficking for sexual servitude and deriving support from trafficking, the Boston Police Department announced in a press release.

title: Ayo Edebiri drops a nostalgic Fung Wah bus reference on ‘Today’ show
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Celebs “The Bear” star shouted out the Boston-to-New York bus reigniting memories of the low-cost ride.
Fung Wah bus Ayo Edebiri just gave a shoutout to a very specific piece of Boston travel lore: the Fung Wah bus.

The Boston-born Emmy-winning actress and comedian, best known for her role as Sydney Adamu on “The Bear,” brought up the now-defunct bus line during a “Today” show appearance Friday promoting her Broadway debut in “Proof.

title: Tufts operating room nurses declare no confidence in leadership
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Local News The nurses are warning that staffing shortages are putting patients and workers at risk.
Suzanne Kreiter / The Boston Globe, File ShareSend this article to your social connections.

The vast majority of the operating room nurses at Tufts Medical Center recently signed a letter of no confidence in leadership, warning that severe staffing shortages are jeopardizing patient safety and straining hospital operations.

title: Wu’s new climate plan sets roadmap for cutting emissions by 2030
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Compared to previous iterations, the new plan emphasizes shorter-term implementation and collaboration with outside partners to cut emissions by 50 percent by 2030.
Boston Mayor Michelle Wu attended a 3-day summit focused on climate change at The Vatican in 2024.
